meta_title: For Freelancers: Git with AI to Solve slow development speed (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome slow development speed. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Git with AI to Solve Slow Dev Speed
content.seo_description: Overcome slow dev cycles by mastering Git with AI. A beginner-friendly case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship client projects faster.
content.answer_block: To eliminate slow development speed, integrate AI agents into your Git workflow. By using Antigravity for automated commit context and Claude Code for intelligent refactoring directly in your terminal, you reduce context switching and manual boilerplate, allowing you to ship professional-grade SaaS features in a fraction of the time.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking on your client's deadline, and you're stuck in a loop of manual boilerplate, repetitive git messages, and syntax debugging. Every minute spent toggling between the editor, the terminal, and documentation is a minute stolen from your profit margin. This is the silent killer of the freelance business model: technical friction.

But the paradigm has shifted. By embedding AI directly into your version control and command-line workflows, you transform your terminal into a high-octane development environment. We are no longer just writing code; we are orchestrating it. Here is how you can leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to regain your speed and dominate your project roadmap.

steps.title: Initialize the Intelligent Repository
steps.description: Establish a base that understands your project architecture before you write a single line of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the existing directory structure and create a project-specific .antigravity configuration that maps out our primary feature modules and tech stack dependencies.

steps.title: Automate Feature Scaffolding
steps.description: Stop manually creating files. Use AI to scaffold entire feature sets based on your technical requirements.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a new feature module for user authentication including controller, service, and test files based on our current architecture pattern.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'scaffold auth module with JWT and role-based access'

steps.title: Context-Aware Git Commits
steps.description: Generate descriptive, professional commit messages that explain the 'why' behind your changes, not just the 'what'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Examine the diffs in the current staging area and draft a concise, conventional-commit formatted message that highlights the business impact of these changes.
steps.claude_code_command: git commit -m "$(claude run 'generate commit message for staged changes')"

steps.title: Automated Code Review & Refactoring
steps.description: Before pushing, ensure your code meets industry standards by running an automated AI audit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the current branch for potential performance bottlenecks, security vulnerabilities, and code style inconsistencies against our project standards.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'review code in src/ and suggest refactoring for performance'

steps.title: Streamlined Deployment Flow
steps.description: Use your AI agent to prepare the final deployment artifacts based on successful tests.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Check all tests, package the production build, and prepare a release summary document for the client based on the latest merge.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'verify build and generate release notes'

pro_tips: Always use a custom system prompt in Claude Code that defines your preferred coding style to ensure consistent output quality.
Use Git hooks to trigger Antigravity analysis automatically before any push, preventing broken code from ever reaching the main branch.
Keep your context window clean by regularly clearing old conversation history in Claude Code to maintain AI focus on the current task.
Treat your commits as documentation; use AI to summarize complex refactors so your clients can easily track project progress.
Leverage the 'dry-run' feature in CLI tools to verify AI-suggested file modifications before they are applied to your disk.
content.next_steps: Apply these techniques to a small, non-critical module in your current project to gain confidence before scaling your workflow across the entire stack.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your development environment and start controlling it. By implementing these AI-driven Git practices, you'll reclaim hours of billable time every week and deliver higher quality results to your clients. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
